steps.title: Compare with random distribution
steps.content: The researchers' claim depends on juvenile plants benefiting from growing near other plants. The baseline is that random distribution would place only about 15% of plants in vegetation patches, so supportive data should exceed that baseline. If juveniles appear in patches far more often than chance predicts, nearby plants likely improve early survival or establishment.

steps.title: Use all species
steps.content: A gives an upper limit without comparing to 15%. B focuses on one species and raw number rather than the pattern. C says two species were below the random expectation, which would weaken the claim. A higher-than-random percentage for every species supports it. The answer is D.
